findings.title: Template snapshot keeps creator's source:'ai' on marked places — friend's first Accept Plan silently DELETES every imported to-do
findings.rootCause: _build_template_snapshot copies the creator's marked_places_json into the snapshot and only runs _clean_marked_places, which pops ONLY dayId and timeOfDay (templates.py:85-109, 182-185). The provenance field `source` is preserved verbatim. Creators build shareable itineraries with the AI planner, so their marked places overwhelmingly carry source:'ai' (stamped by addOrUpdatePlaceFromVerified, markedPlaces.ts:320). That source:'ai' is baked into the snapshot, written straight into the friend's new trip by _instantiate_template (templates.py:208-220, which also does NOT strip source), and hydrated into STATE via fetchTripMedia. On the friend's first Accept Plan, onAcceptPlan calls dropAITaggedPlaces(activeTrip) (useAiPlan.ts:459), which deletes EVERY markedPlace whose source==='ai' (markedPlaces.ts:345-348) — i.e. all the imported template to-dos — before adding the friend's own AI items. This is the precise sibling of the recently-fixed bug where promoting a manual to-do to source:'ai' let dropAITaggedPlaces delete it; the template import path re-introduces source:'ai' items the friend never created.

findings.title: Imported template to-dos are pre-ticked for AI (forAI:true) — friend's very first AI generation silently includes the creator's places in the prompt
findings.rootCause: _clean_marked_places preserves forAI verbatim (only dayId/timeOfDay are popped, templates.py:85-109). useAiPlan builds the to-do prompt suffix from items where p.forAI && p.forManual (useAiPlan.ts:296-298). Imported items carry the creator's forAI:true, so they auto-participate in the friend's first generation with zero user action. This is mild (the to-do page lets the user untick), and arguably intended, but it is an implicit data hand-off the friend didn't choose.
